🏠 Mutual Fund Taxation in India — Complete Data Matrix
FY 2025-26  (AY 2026-27)
Effective under Income-tax Act 2025 + Finance (No. 2) Act 2024 amendments
All rates exclusive of surcharge & 4% Health & Education Cess  |  Units held as capital assets
☰ Capital Gains Taxation Matrix — Fund Category vs Holding Period
Fund Category Equity / Debt Exposure Acquisition Date Holding Period STCG Tax Rate LTCG Tax Rate Key Section & Notes
📈 Equity Funds & Equity-oriented Hybrid
 ≥65%
domestic equity shares
 Any ≤12 months 20% (flat) 12.5%
on gains > ₹1.25 lakh
(no indexation)
₹1.25L exemption; STT applicable
Sec 111A & 112A
STT must be paid on purchase & sale. LTCG exemption ₹1.25 lakh per year.
🏛 Debt / Specified Mutual Funds
 >65%
debt & money market instruments
 On/after 1 Apr 2023 Any period
(deemed STCG)
 Taxed at slab rates No LTCG
Taxed at slab rates
Sec 50AA
Updated definition FY 2025-26. All gains treated as STCG regardless of holding period.
₹ Debt Funds (pre-1 Apr 2023)
 Debt-oriented Before 1 Apr 2023 ≤24 months
>24 months 		Slab rates 12.5%
(no indexation)
Transitional rules only. Grandfathered units bought before 1 Apr 2023.
🔒 ELSS Funds
 Equity-oriented (≥65%) Any 3-year mandatory lock-in Not applicable
(lock-in prevents STCG)
 12.5%
on gains > ₹1.25 lakh (post lock-in)
Sec 80C
Deduction up to ₹1.5 lakh.
Old Regime only.
🎉 Gold ETFs / Silver ETFs (listed)
 Non-equity Any ≤12 months
>12 months 		Slab rates 12.5%
(no indexation)
Treated as non-equity. No STT on purchase.
🌌 Gold Mutual Funds / Gold FoFs
 Non-equity Any ≤24 months
>24 months 		Slab rates 12.5%
(no indexation)
Treated as debt-like. LTCG threshold: >24 months.
🌎 International Funds / FoFs
 Usually <35% domestic equity Any ≤24 months
>24 months 		Slab rates 12.5%
(no indexation)
Treated as non-equity / specified. Gains taxed like debt post-2023 rules.
₹ 1. Dividends
▶ Fully taxable at investor’s income tax slab rate
▶ TDS u/s 194K: 10% if aggregate > ₹10,000 per financial year
▶ No TDS if ≤ ₹5,000 in some cases (verify latest)
📈 2. STT (Securities Transaction Tax)
▶ Only on Equity-oriented funds
▶ Rate: 0.001% on sale/redemption on recognised stock exchange
▶ Not applicable on debt, liquid, gold (non-equity) or dividends
💲 3. Surcharge & Cess
▶ Health & Education Cess: 4% on (tax + surcharge)
▶ Surcharge on capital gains (u/s 111A/112/112A): capped at 15%
▶ New Tax Regime (115BAC): surcharge capped at 25%
🌍 1. NRI Taxation
Equity STCG 20%
Equity LTCG 12.5%
LTCG exemption > ₹1.25L
Debt / Specified Slab rates
TDS Rate 20% or DTAA
▶ Tax Residency Certificate + Form 10F mandatory for DTAA benefits
▶ Sec 206AA/206AB: higher TDS if PAN/ITR not filed (exceptions apply)
📅 2. SIP / SWP
▶ SIP: FIFO method — older units (>12 months equity) → LTCG 12.5%; recent <12 months → 20%
▶ SWP: Every withdrawal taxed as redemption (FIFO)
🏢 3. Corporate Investors
Optional regime u/s 115BAA 22%
New manufacturing u/s 115BAB 15%
Small cos (turnover ≤ ₹400cr, FY 2022-23) 25%
⚡ Quick Reference
Equity STCG 20%
Equity LTCG 12.5%
Debt (post Apr 23) Slab
ELSS lock-in 3 years
Health & Ed. Cess 4%
Surcharge cap (LTCG) 15%
⚠ Disclaimer: This information is for educational purposes only and reflects rules as of FY 2025-26. Tax laws change frequently. Consult a qualified tax advisor or CA before making investment decisions. Rates shown are exclusive of applicable surcharge and 4% Health & Education Cess.
